title: Heuristic Filtering
concept: Heuristic filters check document length, symbol-to-word ratios, and repetitive patterns to prune out templated boilerplate and spam text.

exercises.prompt: What is the main goal of filtering goal of using heuristic filters on a web dataset cleaning: Why dataset

options.text: To make the AI run slower
To clean out spam and junk text before training
To add advertisements into the text

exercises.explain: Before training an AI, we must clean the data. Heuristic filters help us remove junk text like spam or broken code quickly.

cards.title: Cleaning Up the Mess
cards.body: If you feed an AI training dataset full of gibberish, spam, and broken web templates, the AI will learn to talk like a broken computer. How do we clean up billions of web pages without reading them all ourselves? We use smart rules of thumb!

cards.title: What is Heuristic Filtering?
cards.body: Heuristic filtering uses quick, practical 'rules of thumb' to scan text documents. It looks at simple clues—like whether a document is too short, has too many weird symbols, or repeats the same sentences—to decide if the text is junk or high-quality.

cards.title: The Junk Mail Sorter
cards.body: Think of a heuristic filter like a mail carrier sorting out junk flyers. Instead of reading every single word of your mail, they quickly throw away envelopes that are way too light, have zero text, or contain the exact same printed coupon copied 50 times. It is a set of quick check rules!

cards.title: Filtering in Action
cards.body: Imagine a web page that only says 'Click here! Click here!' 100 times, or a page containing nothing but code symbols like '###$$$%%%'. A heuristic filter will instantly spot these weird patterns and delete them before they can confuse our AI model.

cards.title: Do they actually 'Read'?
cards.body: People think heuristic filters use super-smart AI brainpower to understand the meaning of every sentence. In reality, they are just fast, simple rules—like counting the number of words or checking if a page has too many exclamation points!

cards.title: Clean vs. Cluttered Text
cards.body: See how heuristic filtering separates the clean, readable text from repetitive web boilerplate and symbol clutter.

right.text: "$$$ FREE! $$$ Click here! Click here! Click here! $$$ FREE! $$$"

left.text: "The history of space exploration began in the mid-20th century with rockets..."

exercises.prompt: Why does a heuristic filter look at the ratio of symbols to words in a text?

options.text: It means the text is highly poetic and creative.
It suggests the text might be junk, code, or broken formatting.
It guarantees the text is written by a professional.
It means the document is too long.

exercises.explain: If a document has a very high ratio of symbols (like %, $, #) compared to normal words, it is likely computer code or garbage text, not helpful language for the AI.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Heuristic filters are designed to be fast and simple rather than deep and slow.
exercises.explain: Heuristic filters use simple, fast rules like word counts and symbol ratios, making them extremely fast at cleaning massive datasets.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the explanation of heuristic rules.
exercises.sentence: A heuristic filter checks simple properties like document status and text length to clean data.
exercises.explain: Heuristic filters look at simple traits like document length to instantly spot and remove empty pages or short snippets of spam.

exercises.prompt:
Compare these two text samples:
Text 1: "Learning to cook requires patience, fresh ingredients, and practice."
Text 2: "Buy now. Click here. Buy now. Click here. Buy now. Click here."
Which one would a heuristic filter flag as junk?

options.text: Text 1 is junk because it contains lowercase letters.
Text 2 is junk because it repeats the exact same sentence pattern.

exercises.explain: The second text is just a repeating spam pattern (a template), which is exactly what a heuristic filter is designed to detect and remove.

exercises.prompt: Why do developers use heuristic filtering when preparing text datasets for AI training?

options.text: To translate documents into multiple languages automatically
To make sure every document is exactly the same length
To quickly filter out spam, templates, and low-quality text using simple rules
To write new creative stories for AI datasets

exercises.explain: Heuristic filters are designed to quickly throw away bad data (like spam, blank pages, and repeated text) using fast rules of thumb, saving clean and high-quality data for training AI models.
